_meta.spec: IDTA 02004 Handover Documentation, Version 2.0, June 2025 (based on VDI 2770 Blatt 1:2020)
_meta.source: IDTA-02004-2-0_Submodel_Handover-Documentation.pdf — element tables §2.4–2.9 (Tables 2–14) + classification tables (Table 1, Table 16), copied verbatim from the bundled text extraction (IDTA-02004-2-0_Submodel_Handover-Documentation.txt); the two printing anomalies below were re-verified against the PDF binary with pypdf

schema.primary: the semanticId printed in the table (ECLASS IRDI, ECLASS pair IRDI, or admin-shell.io IRI); 'VERIFY-IN-PDF' where the value is not printed/legible
schema.supplemental: the supplementalSemanticId(s) printed in the table — string, array where the PDF prints multiple (Document), or null where none is printed; the eclass-cdp.com URLs are the printed supplementals (pattern: '#'→'-' under https://api.eclass-cdp.com/)
schema.modelType: AAS modelType (Property | MultiLanguageProperty | File | ReferenceElement | Entity | SubmodelElementCollection | SubmodelElementList)
schema.cardinality: as printed: 1 | 0..1 | 1..* | 0..* ; 'VERIFY-IN-PDF' where the spec prints no cardinality row for the element
schema.parent: idShort of the containing element ('HandoverDocumentation' = submodel root)

warnings: OrganizationShortName prints ONLY 'https://api.eclass-cdp.com/0173-1-02-ABI002-003' as its semanticId — no ECLASS IRDI, no 'supplementalSemanticId:' prefix. Verified in the PDF binary (page 14): this is printed in the spec itself, not an extraction artifact. Every sibling prints an IRDI primary + cdp supplemental; the IRDI expected by pattern would be 0173-1#02-ABI002#003 — VERIFY against the official 2.0 template AASX before hard-coding either form into a conformance gate (Annex C change #8 touched exactly the ECLASS definitions in this area).

Document's first supplementalSemanticId prints as '0173-1#02-ABI500#003~0/0173-1#01-AHF579#003' — the '~0' infix is genuinely printed in the PDF (verified in binary, page 10). Copied verbatim; do not 'fix' it without checking the official AASX.

No details tables exist for the DocumentIds / DocumentClassifications / DocumentVersions SMLs — the cardinality of the DocumentId / DocumentClassification / DocumentVersion SMCs inside them is NOT printed (1..* is implied by the set semantics and §2.1, e.g. 'Multiple DocumentVersions can be assigned to each Document'), and their orderRelevant / typeValueListElement attributes are likewise unprinted. Marked 'VERIFY-IN-PDF' — confirm against the official 2.0 AASX.

The Language SML's child Property prints idShort 'en' (Table 9) — a per-language placeholder; the language itself is the Property VALUE (ISO 639 code, example 'en'). Under metamodel 3.0, AASd-120 forbids idShort on direct SML children; the template AASX is migrated to metamodel V3.01 (Annex C change #4). The printed idShorts of ALL SML children in this spec (Document, DocumentId, en, DigitalFile, RefersToEntity, ...) are table readability only.

The submodel semanticId is an ECLASS IRDI (0173-1#01-AHF578#003), NOT an admin-shell.io IRI. The v1.x lineage used https://admin-shell.io/vdi/2770/1/0/... style IRIs (only EntitiesForDocumentation / EntityForDocumentation / DocumentedEntities / DocumentedEntity keep that namespace in 2.0). The 1.2 submodel semanticId is NOT printed in this PDF — VERIFY-IN-1-2-PDF for legacy compatibility work.

ALL ECLASS IRDIs were re-keyed to ECLASS Release 15 in 2.0 (Annex C change #2, non-backward compatible). 1.2→2.0 renames (also Nc): DocumentVersionId→Version, Summary→Description, ValueId→DocumentIdentifier.
Table 1 prints the DE class name for 03-01 as 'Montage, Demontage' against EN 'Commissioning, de-commissioning' — copied verbatim (spec-table quirk).

Annex E uses 1.x-era attribute names 'DocumentClassificationSystem' / 'DocumentClassId' when describing IEC 61355 usage — the 2.0 idShorts are ClassificationSystem / ClassId (editorial leftover in the spec).

Documents.note: orderRelevant=No, typeValueListElement=SubmodelElementCollection, semanticIdListElement=[GlobalReference, 0173-1#02-ABI500#003/0173-1#01-AHF579#003] (Table 3) — every child Document's semanticId must match it (AASd-107)

Document.note: one SMC per VDI 2770 Document; the '~0' in the first supplemental is printed verbatim in the PDF (see _meta warning); a document existing in multiple languages = DIFFERENT Documents (§2.1)

Entity.note: marks a sub-part (e.g. supplier part) important for the documentation; recommendation: co-managed for simple cases, self-managed (with globalAssetId, AASd-14) when the part has its own AAS; Entity elements may contain SubmodelElements but no self-standing Submodels (footnote 3, §2.9)

DocumentIds.note: set of document identifiers; one ID in this collection should be used as a preferred ID; orderRelevant/typeValueListElement not printed (no details table) — VERIFY-IN-PDF

DocumentClassifications.note: classification per VDI 2770 Blatt 1:2020 is MANDATORY (§2.3); additional systems (e.g. IEC 61355-1:2008) may be added; orderRelevant/typeValueListElement not printed — VERIFY-IN-PDF

DocumentVersions.note: orderRelevant/typeValueListElement not printed (no details table) — VERIFY-IN-PDF

DocumentedEntities.note: orderRelevant=No, typeValueListElement=ReferenceElement (Table 14); used when a Document relates to a dependent (self- or co-managed) Entity and not to the Asset of the AAS itself (§2.2)

DocumentId.note: cardinality inside the DocumentIds SML not printed (1..* implied — 'set of document identifiers', at least one); identifies the Document within a given Domain; simplified tuple of domain ID + document ID (Annex D §6)

DocumentDomainId.note: domain in which the DocumentId is unique, e.g. name or acronym of the providing organisation

DocumentIdentifier.note: alphanumeric character sequence uniquely identifying a document; was idShort 'ValueId' in 1.2 (Annex C change #9, Nc)

DocumentIsPrimary.note: flags the preferred/'primary' identifier when at least two DocumentIds exist (commonly the asset owner's view)

DocumentClassification.note: cardinality inside the SML not printed (1..* implied — the VDI 2770 classification is mandatory); a Document can carry multiple classifications in one system and across systems (§2.3)

ClassId.note: unique ID of the document class within the classification system; for VDI 2770 Blatt 1:2020 use the 12 ClassIds in _meta.classifications; for IEC 61355-1:2008 a two-letter upper-case code (Annex E)

ClassName.note: name of the class in the classification system; multiple languages allowed, EN is MANDATORY (§2.3)

ClassificationSystem.note: identification of the classification system; EXACT value for the mandatory system: 'VDI 2770 Blatt 1:2020' (§2.3); for the optional IEC system: 'IEC 61355-1:2008' (Annex E)

DocumentVersion.note: cardinality inside the SML not printed (1..* implied); shall contain at least one DigitalFile (§2.1); multiple versions of the same document = multiple DocumentVersion instances; multiple languages WITHIN one document = ONE DocumentVersion with multiple Language entries

Language.note: orderRelevant=No, typeValueListElement=Property (Table 9); language style of the document

en.note: PLACEHOLDER idShort printed in Table 9 — the actual content is the Property VALUE (ISO 639 language code, example 'en'); at least one language Property per DocumentVersion; under metamodel 3.0 SML children carry NO idShort (AASd-120)

Version.note: was idShort 'DocumentVersionId' in 1.2 (Annex C change #6, Nc, new semanticId)

Subtitle.note: list of language-dependent subtitles of the document

Description.note: plain text characterizing the content of the document; was idShort 'Summary' in 1.2 (Annex C change #7, Nc, new semanticId)

KeyWords.note: list of language-dependent keywords of the document

StatusSetDate.note: date when the document status was set

StatusValue.note: document lifecycle milestone; the following two values should be used: 'InReview' (under review), 'Released' (released)

OrganizationShortName.note: PRINTING ANOMALY (verified in the PDF binary): only the eclass-cdp URL is printed as the semanticId — no IRDI, no supplemental. Expected IRDI by pattern: 0173-1#02-ABI002#003 — VERIFY against the official 2.0 AASX before gating on either form. Short name of the organization (author of the document)

OrganizationOfficialName.note: official name of the organization of the author of the document

RefersToEntities.note: orderRelevant=No, typeValueListElement=ReferenceElement (Table 10); generic refers-to relationship to another document/document version (loose relationship); references may span multiple AAS — the AssetId shall be used as first key (§2.2)

BasedOnReferences.note: orderRelevant=No, typeValueListElement=ReferenceElement (Table 11); based-on relationship (strong), e.g. content based on specification requirements

DigitalFiles.note: orderRelevant=No, typeValueListElement=File (Table 13); MIME-Type, file name and file contents given by the File SubmodelElement; every DocumentVersion shall contain at least one DigitalFile (§2.1)

DigitalFile.note: VDI 2770 requires PDF/A (ISO 19005-1/-2/-3 = PDF/A-1/-2/-3); may alternatively be provided via a link (external URL in the File value) but the linked document and the ISO 19005 file should be identical (legal requirements, e.g. Machinery Directive); multiple DigitalFiles with different MIME types must each represent the DocumentVersion IN TOTAL with equal content (§2.1)

PreviewFile.note: DIRECT child of DocumentVersion (not inside DigitalFiles); preview image of the DocumentVersion, e.g. first page, in a commonly used image format and low resolution

DocumentedEntity.note: reference to the documented Entity — used when the Document relates to a dependent (self- or co-managed) Entity and not to the Asset of the AAS itself (§2.2)

vdi2770Blatt1_2020.source: Table 1 (§2.3), repeated in Annex B — copied verbatim

iec61355_2008.source: Table 16 (Annex E) — a SELECTION; the full range of two-letter codes of IEC 61355-1:2008 may be used; ClassId = two-letter upper-case code
iec61355_2008.note: Annex E phrases this with the 1.x names 'DocumentClassificationSystem'/'DocumentClassId'; the 2.0 idShorts are ClassificationSystem/ClassId

classes.BG: Site planning and site organization documents
classes.LH: In-building location documents (also applied for ships, aircraft, etc.)
classes.PD: Product lists and product type lists
